call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Financial Solutions. I see you filled out a form online regarding our Signature Credit Card. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card at a rate of 15.99%. It has some fantastic rewards, like 2% cashback on all purchases. Does that sound interesting? 
Eleanor: Hmm, maybe. What's the annual fee? 
Agent: It's $99 a year, but honestly, with the cashback, most customers more than make that back. We also have travel insurance and purchase protection. 
Eleanor: Travel insurance? That's good. I do travel a bit. 
Agent: Exactly! And it's all bundled in. We can get you approved in just a few minutes. Just need to confirm a few details. Your income is around $6500 a month, correct? 
Eleanor: Yes, that's right. 
Agent: Perfect. And you're currently employed? 
Eleanor: Yes. 
Agent: Wonderful. Okay, Eleanor, I'm processing your application now... and… you're approved! We'll send over the card in 7-10 business days. Is there anything else I can help you with today? 
Eleanor: No, I think that's it. Thank you. 
Agent: You're very welcome! Have a great day.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Financial Solutions. We're offering a pre-approved Personal Loan. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't ask for this. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… we acquired your information through a marketing partner. We're offering a $10,000 Personal Loan at 18.5%. 
Robert: 18.5?! That's highway robbery! I already have a loan with my bank. 
Agent: Well, sir, we can potentially offer a lower rate if we look at your credit profile. 
Robert: I don't need you to look at my credit profile. I'm perfectly happy with my bank. And frankly, I'm a little annoyed you called me out of the blue. 
Agent: I understand, sir. I apologize for the inconvenience. 
Robert: Just take my number off your list. 
Agent: Certainly, sir. I'll make a note of that. Have a good day. 
Robert: *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Financial Solutions. I'm calling about our Signature Credit Card. I understand you were referred by a partner. 
Jessica: Oh, yeah, my friend Sarah mentioned it. 
Agent: Great! So, we're offering a $3,000 Signature Credit Card at 16.49%. It's a great card for building credit and has some good rewards. 
Jessica: Rewards? Like what? 
Agent: We have 1.5% cashback on all purchases, and bonus rewards on dining and travel. 
Jessica: Okay, that sounds… decent. I'm self-employed, though. Will that be a problem? 
Agent: Not necessarily. We just need to verify your income. Do you have any documentation, like tax returns? 
Jessica: Uh, yeah, I think so. I'd have to look. 
Agent: No problem. We can also look at bank statements. It's pretty flexible. 
Jessica: Hmm. I'm kinda busy right now. Can you send me some information by email? 
Agent: Absolutely! I can send you a brochure with all the details, including a link to apply online. What's your email address? 
Jessica: It's jessica.chen@email.com. 
Agent: Perfect. I'll send that over right away. Thanks for your time, Jessica. 
Jessica: Thanks.

Agent: Good afternoon, Mr. Davis. This is Lisa from Financial Solutions. I see you requested information about our Personal Loans online. 
Michael: Yes, I did. I'm looking to consolidate some debt. 
Agent: Excellent. We can definitely help with that. We're pre-approving you for a $20,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 12.99%. 
Michael: That sounds pretty good. What are the terms? 
Agent: We offer terms from 3 to 7 years. What's your preferred repayment period? 
Michael: Probably 5 years. 
Agent: Okay. So, your monthly payment would be approximately $392. Now, there is an origination fee of 1% of the loan amount, which would be $200. 
Michael: An origination fee? That's… annoying. 
Agent: I understand. It covers the cost of processing the loan. However, the overall savings from consolidating your debt at a lower interest rate will likely outweigh the fee. 
Michael: Hmm. What other fees are there? 
Agent: There are no prepayment penalties. If you pay off the loan early, you won't be charged anything extra. There's also a late payment fee of $25 if a payment is more than 15 days late. 
Michael: Okay. So, $200 origination fee, $25 late fee… and 12.99%? Is that the best rate you can offer? 
Agent: That is the initial rate based on your credit profile. We can potentially review it if you provide additional documentation. 
Michael: Alright, let's proceed with the application then. 
Agent: Wonderful! Let's get started...

Agent: Hello, Ms. Rodriguez? This is Kevin calling from Financial Solutions. We're offering a pre-approved Personal Loan. 
Patricia: (Yelling) You guys called me *yesterday*! I told you I'm not interested! Where did you even get my number?! 
Agent: Ma'am, I apologize for the inconvenience. We're offering a $5,000 Personal Loan at 22.99%. 
Patricia: 22.99?! Are you kidding me?! I'm on a fixed income! I can't afford that! 
Agent: We can discuss the terms… 
Patricia: No! I want my number removed from your list *immediately*! This is harassment! 
Agent: I understand, ma'am. I'll… 
Patricia: *click*
